title: EEOC Title VII of the Civil Rights Act
description: Prohibits employment discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin. Applies to employers with 15+ employees. Covers hiring, firing, promotion, compensation, and all terms of employment.
requirements: No discrimination in job postings or hiring criteria based on protected classes
Maintain records of applicant demographics for EEOC reporting
Reasonable accommodation for religious practices
Anti-retaliation protections for employees who file complaints
EEO-1 reporting for employers with 100+ employees
penalties: Back pay, compensatory damages up to $300K, punitive damages, injunctive relief, attorney fees. Pattern-or-practice suits by EEOC.

title: GDPR - Recruitment Data Processing
description: General Data Protection Regulation requirements for processing candidate personal data during recruitment. Requires lawful basis, data minimization, and candidate rights including right to erasure.
requirements: Obtain explicit consent or establish legitimate interest for processing candidate data
Provide privacy notice before collecting personal data
Data minimization - collect only what is necessary for recruitment
Right to access, rectification, and erasure of candidate data
Data Protection Impact Assessment for large-scale processing
Appoint Data Protection Officer if processing at scale
72-hour breach notification to supervisory authority
Cross-border transfer restrictions (Standard Contractual Clauses required)
penalties: Up to 4% of annual global turnover or EUR 20 million, whichever is greater. Administrative fines, orders to cease processing.

title: Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) - Employment
description: Prohibits discrimination against qualified individuals with disabilities in all aspects of employment. Requires reasonable accommodations unless undue hardship. Applies to employers with 15+ employees.
requirements: Job postings must focus on essential functions, not physical requirements unless essential
Reasonable accommodation during application and interview process
Cannot ask about disability status before conditional job offer
Interactive process to determine reasonable accommodations
Accessible application systems (WCAG 2.1 AA for online portals)
Medical exams only after conditional offer, must be job-related
penalties: Back pay, compensatory damages up to $300K, injunctive relief, reasonable accommodation orders, attorney fees.

title: Colorado Equal Pay for Equal Work Act (SB 19-085)
description: Requires employers to disclose compensation and benefits in job postings for positions that could be performed in Colorado. Prohibits pay history inquiries.
requirements: Include salary range or hourly rate in all job postings
Disclose benefits information in job postings
Notify current employees of promotion opportunities
Maintain job description and wage rate records for each position
Cannot ask about salary history or rely on it for compensation decisions
penalties: Fine of $500-$10,000 per violation. Private right of action with compensatory and punitive damages.

title: California Pay Transparency Act (SB 1162)
description: Requires employers with 15+ employees to include pay scale in job postings. Employers with 100+ employees must file annual pay data reports including median and mean hourly rates by race/ethnicity and sex.
requirements: Include pay scale (salary or hourly wage range) in all job postings
Provide pay scale to current employees for their role upon request
File annual pay data report with Civil Rights Department
Maintain records of job titles and wage rate history for 3 years
Cannot ask about salary history
penalties: $100-$10,000 per violation. No penalty for first violation if corrected. Injunctive relief and civil penalties.

title: New York Pay Transparency Law
description: Requires employers with 4+ employees to disclose compensation range in job advertisements for positions performed in New York. Covers internal and external postings.
requirements: Include minimum and maximum salary in job advertisements
Applies to jobs performed in whole or in part within New York
Covers promotions and transfer opportunities posted internally
Cannot retaliate against employees who inquire about or discuss wages
Maintain records of compensation ranges for each position
penalties: Civil penalties up to $250,000 for violations. Orders to comply, compensatory damages.

title: Washington Equal Pay and Opportunities Act (SB 5761)
description: Requires employers with 15+ employees to include wage scale or salary range and benefits description in job postings. Applies to positions that could be performed in Washington.
requirements: Include wage scale or salary range in all job postings
Include general description of benefits and other compensation
Applies to postings by employers and third-party recruiters
Provide current employees with wage scale for their position upon request
Cannot seek salary history from applicants
penalties: Actual damages, statutory damages up to $5,000, attorney fees. Washington Department of Labor enforcement.

title: Age Discrimination in Employment Act (ADEA)
description: Prohibits employment discrimination against individuals 40 years of age or older. Applies to employers with 20+ employees. Covers hiring, firing, promotion, compensation, terms, and conditions of employment.
requirements: No age-based criteria in job postings (avoid 'digital native', 'recent graduate')
Cannot set age limits for hiring unless bona fide occupational qualification
Interview questions must not target age or graduation dates
Equal training and development opportunities regardless of age
No mandatory retirement except for specific executive positions
Job advertisements cannot express age preference or limitation
penalties: Back pay, liquidated damages (double back pay for willful violations), injunctive relief, attorney fees. No compensatory or punitive damages.
